name: The Power Of Pranic Self-healing For Women - Pranic Healing Session
description:
A Woman Healed Is A Woman Empowered. A Woman Healed Is A Force Unleashed.
Pranic Healing is a revolutionary no-touch, no-drug tested energy-based treatment that uses Prana, Chi or Energy to improve a wide range of physical & psychological issues.
Learn About The Power Of Pranic Self-healing For Women - Get to know how easily, effortlessly, rapidly and properly, you, as a woman can,

Restore Your Health & Harmony.
Build Resilience & Reconnect with Your Inner Strength.
Achieve Success & Take Charge Of Destiny.
Questions & Answers.
Healing Demonstration.

Age: 16+

name: El disparo/One Shot - Theatre Performance
description:
On World Theatre Day, TheatreSHIft presents a script-in-hand reading of El disparo by Uruguayan playwright Estela Golovchenko, translated into English as The Shot by Stephen Brown.
Part of TheatreSHIft’s Not Lost in Translation rehearsed reading series, the play offers a compelling window into a distant world where personal histories collide with present day reality. The narrative follows a woman who, upon discovering an intruder in her home, must navigate a precarious balance between truth and deception to survive.
In a charged environment where anything can happen, they must both make choices between what they conceal and reveal? Inventive in form, the play takes the audience on an unpredictable journey, with surprises at every turn.
 
El disparo/One Shot, Script in hand reading
by Estela Golovchenko/Stephen Brown (trans)
Directed by Mayura Baweja
Performers: Swati De and Paarth Maheshwari
Production: Mrinali Chowhan and Norman Leslie V Doss
Poster: Sanjana Baweja and Mrinali Chowhan

Age: 16+

name: The Sports Page - A 6-month Sports Writing Workshop
description:
Six months. Six Saturdays. One Sports community.
Great sports writing isn’t just about the score—it’s about the heartbeat of the game, the psychology of the athlete, and the drama of the final second. This March, join us for an intensive, 6-month journey into the high-stakes world of sports journalism and storytelling.
On the last Saturday morning of every month (11:00 AM – 1:00 PM), we are bringing together a squad of aspiring and seasoned writers to master the craft of the "Long Read," the match report, and the athlete profile. 
This workshop will have 4 stages of writing and 2 masterclass sections.
The masterclasses will feature renowned sports writers N. Sudarshan and Prajwal Hegde sharing their insights.

About the Facilitator:

Dr. Vijay Krishnamurthy is a Sports Researcher (Ph.D.) and an 
Executive Coach (PCC) with 28 years of global experience across 
technology design, sports research, and leadership development. 
Vijay is the co-author of the book "Get Kids To Play,” and has also 
published research articles in leading academic journals, including 
Ivey and Harvard Business Publishing.
Age Group: 18+

name: Brand Basics for First-Time Entrepreneurs - Entrepreneurship Workshop
description:
For the first time, First Brick Collective is bringing together two different but equally important pieces of the entrepreneurial journey: shaping your idea and understanding how you communicate it.
Sudhir Kiran will be walking participants through the basics of building a brand and finding the right words to explain what they’re building, and Shravan will help them understand their natural communication style and how it influences the way they lead, pitch, and build relationships.
Two founders. Two frameworks. One empowering afternoon.
Limited seats. Link in bio.
 
Age: 18 and above
performer: by Sudhir Kiran & First Brick Collective

name: DIY Leather Journal Workshop - Workshop
description:
In a time when most experiences are fast, digital, and fleeting, HARTS Lifestyle & Gifting is bringing back the joy of creating by hand. On 28 March, HARTS will host a DIY Leather Journal Crafting Workshop at Atta Galatta, inviting participants to slow down and experience the process of crafting something personal and meaningful.
The workshop is designed as an immersive, hands-on experience where participants will assemble their own leather journals from scratch. Guided by the HARTS team, attendees will learn the basics of journal construction, select their preferred journal size and leather cover, and personalise the final piece with their name or initials.
The DIY Leather Journal Workshop continues HARTS’ growing series of creative experiences that blend craft, mindfulness and creating by hands.
All materials included. No prior experience required.

Age: 7-18 years

name: Find The Light That Hides In You - Book Signing
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for a special book signing of Find the Light That Hides in You by Ruchira Puri Pujari on Saturday, 28th March, from 3:00 PM to 4:00 PM.
In Find the Light that Hides in You: A Compassionate Enquiry, Ruchira Puri Pujari traces her deeply personal and mystical journey through past lives and present-day wounds, uncovering the quiet, powerful reservoirs of healing that reside within us all. Blending memoir with spiritual reflection, she explores how pain can become a portal to self-realisation, and how the light of the soul remains untouched by trauma.
Tender and introspective, the book is an invitation to turn inward with courage, curiosity, and compassion—to reconnect with one’s innate joy, worth, and truth. A luminous meditation on emotional, spiritual, and karmic healing, it reminds us that awakening is not about seeking the light outside, but remembering it within.
Readers are invited to meet the author, get their copies signed, and engage in an intimate, informal interaction.

Age: 18+

name: Atta Galatta presents “The BWW Short Story Community” - Literary Meetup
description:
The BWW Short Fiction Community at Atta Galatta will discuss
Lara’s Theme
By Madhuri Vijay
Set in Bangalore and narrated by thirteen-year-old Kushal, Lara’s Theme offers a sharp and tender look at the quiet dramas of family life. As Kushal observes his hobby-hopping mother, saxophone-playing father, and brilliant older brother Tarun, the story explores the small rivalries, ambitions, and insecurities that shape how people see themselves—and how deeply the desire to feel exceptional runs through us all.
Read the text and come for the event.

name: Imagining Worlds - An open discussion on sci-fi/fantasy fiction
description:
Nothing evokes your sense of wonder as much as opening a book and finding a map on the first page. Fantasy and Science Fiction as a genre is growing rapidly in India, and raises crucial and fascinating questions: What lures us to secondary worlds? What according to you makes a fantasy story feel distinctly Indian? Do we rely too much on mythology, or is there room for entirely new worlds inspired by our cultures, cities, and futures? Join Prashanth Srivatsa, author of The Spice Gate, for an open discussion on the present and future of Indian speculative fiction. This informal round table, think of it like our very own Council of Elrond, will explore worldbuilding, storytelling, and the many possibilities for fantasy and sci-fi in India. Whether you're an avid reader, an aspiring writer, or simply curious about the genre and its myriad imaginary worlds, come join the conversation.
Age: 18+

name: Atta Galatta presents “Let Poetry Be” - Poetry Open Mic
description:
This monthly gathering will see poetry coming to life, through reading, music, art and any other forms of artistic expression, at Atta Galatta. Listeners and performers are all welcome!
Date: 28th March
Time: 4pm 
Venue: Atta Galatta, Indiranagar
 
Event specifics & submission guidelines –
1. Theme - Urban Tales (any original work)
2. Works can be original or of other poets, performed live
3. All languages welcome
4. Participants can read to music (acoustic instrument/s), dramatise, use props & costumes
5. Each participant will enjoy 5 minutes of reading time
6. Kindly submit your poem and name to letpoetrybe@gmail.com on or before 26th Mar midnight. WE WILL NOT ACCEPT ATTACHMENTS OR LINKS. SUBJECT OF YOUR MAIL MUST READ AS 'Entry for-February LPB meet-up'
7. You may also send entries that are not related to the theme
8. The event will begin at 4pm sharp and will be held at Atta Galatta. Participants to report not later than 3:45 pm to hear about performance order and meet the host
9. For any further clarifications, write to us at letpoetrybe@gmail.com
You are now welcome to flood our inbox!

name: Unspoken: A Language Of The Eyes - An Evening Of Expression
description:
An intimate 60-minute interactive experience exploring everyday human emotions through the expressive language of classical art form.
Designed for café spaces, this session blends classical Abhinaya with modern emotional storytelling in a conversational, accessible format.
Open to all — no dance background required.
A curated cultural evening that brings community and expression in a meaningful way.

name: Bangalore Storytelling Society - A story telling meetup
description:
Bangalore Storytelling Society is hosting a family extravaganza for World Storytelling Day. Just like stories are for everyone, this curated performance is designed for an interactive, wholesome experience for friends and families. Our handpicked tellers have poignant, reflective, energetic,  quirky bags of stories that blend and blur the boundaries of age.
Join us on March 29th, 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM at Atta Galatta. Book your tickets as we turn ‘Light into Dark” , one story at a time.

name: Fineline Handwriting Workshop - Improve Your Penmanship
description:
There are five main types of handwriting among which Cursive and Lucida are popular styles for enhancing penmanship.
This workshop will help the children to understand different types of handwriting and choose the best fonts for labelling subject-based diagrams, charts, illustrations, graphs, drawings, schematics, figures, and representations. A neat presentation is key to good marks. 
Registration limited to 15 seats. All Materials Provided. Participants need to carry with them the most recently used pen.

Age: 10-16 yrs

name: Brachio - An Interactive Dramatic Reading
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for an interactive dramatic reading by Venkataraghavan, where the author brings his story to life by performing it as a lively play.
 
About the Book:
Brachio lives by the beach, loves helping people and is very, very tall.
But what will he do when the village-folk no longer need him?
 
As he embarks on a remarkable journey, Brachio becomes a star, gets hit by
fluffy balls and makes a great escape!
Will Brachio ever discover what it truly means to be tall?
 
About the Author:
Venkataraghavan is so tall that he can touch most ceilings. He was the first person to receive the NLF Fellowship for Children's Book Creators.
Age: 5-10 yrs

name: Guided Painting Workshop  - Art session
description:
Paint and vibe your way through a cozy afternoon with us 💛
Join The Healing Palette for a guided painting session where you’ll create your own canvas artwork with acrylics, no experience needed just good energy and a love for trying something new.
We’ll guide you step-by-step and make sure you walk away with a piece you’re proud of (and maybe a new favorite hobby 👀)
 
Event Details:
🗓 29th March
⏰ 1:30 PM – 3:30 PM
📍 Atta Galatta, Bangalore
 
🎨 All materials provided
🥤 Light refreshments included
Come for the art, stay for the vibes, leave with a masterpiece 🎨✨
Age: 18+

name: Behind the Big Screen - Book Launch
description:
Bengaluru Book Event: “Behind the Big Screen” with Master Manjunath, Sunanda Mehta, and Suchitra Iyer on Sunday, March 29, 2026.
Atta Galatta art gallery in Indiranagar, Bengaluru, will host an engaging literary and cinematic evening titled “Behind the Big Screen” from 4:00 p.m. to 5:00 p.m.
The event features a special conversation with renowned child actor Master Manjunath (beloved for his role in the iconic TV series Malgudi Days) alongside authors Sunanda Mehta and Suchitra Iyer, who will discuss their new book, Behind the Big Screen: The Untold Stories of Bollywood’s Child Actors (published by Bloomsbury). The invigorating session will be moderated by Chiteisri Devi, an avid reader and a connoisseur of all things art.
The book delves into the lesser-known lives of Bollywood’s former child stars—beyond the fame and applause—exploring the challenges, the highs, personal struggles, exploitation risks, and lasting impacts of growing up in front of the camera. Drawing on exclusive interviews with actors, filmmakers, and psychologists, it offers poignant insights into innocence lost and experience gained.
Attendees can look forward to:
* Readings of excerpts from the book
* A lively discussion on the realities of child stardom
* An interactive Q&A session with Master Manjunath and the authors
This nostalgic yet eye-opening event is perfect for film enthusiasts, literature lovers, and anyone curious about the hidden stories behind India’s silver screen legends. Join to celebrate iconic childhood performances while uncovering the human stories that continued long after the cameras stopped rolling.
performer: by  Sunanda Mehta and Suchitra Iyer

name: Read A Kitaab - Book Club
description:
Bangalore, we’re back — and this time we’re crossing borders through stories. 🌍📚
Join us for the Read A Kitaab Bangalore Monthly Meet as we celebrate powerful voices, translated journeys, and stories that travel across languages and cultures. ✨
👩🏽‍💻👵🏾👩🏻‍🎓 From Nobel laureates to hidden gems, from quiet introspection to fierce resistance — Women in Translation reminds us that great literature speaks every language.
📍 Location: Atta Galatta, Indira Nagar
📅 Date: Sunday, 29th March
⏰ Time: 5:00 PM – 7:00 PM
☕ Expect warm conversations, new bookish friendships, and a room full of readers who believe stories can change the world.
✨ Entry is free. Registration is mandatory.
 
Come alone or bring a fellow book lover — either way, you’ll leave with a longer TBR and a fuller heart. 💛📖
#ReadAKitaab #BangaloreReaders #WomenInTranslation

name: Music of Mystics - An Evening of Sufi, Qawwali & Ghazals
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for Music of Mystics, an evening dedicated to Sufi, Qawwali and Ghazals, led by Hindustani vocalist Ustad Ashraf Hydroz, Kerala Sangeetha Nataka Akademi Awardee.
Rooted in the Sufi understanding of Ishq — a love that goes beyond the self — and Noor, the light believed to reside within every heart, the concert reflects on longing, surrender and remembrance through music. The repertoire moves between traditional Qawwalis, reflective Sufi compositions and timeless Ghazals, allowing poetry and melody to unfold at their own pace.
Accompanied by Kabeer (Harmonium), Ullas (SaiBanjo), Sirajudheen  (Dholak) and Dileep Vikram  (Tabla), the ensemble brings together classical structure and emotional depth in an intimate live setting
 
March 29 | 6 PM
Age: All
Atta Galatta, Indiranagar, Bengaluru

name: Your Stick Will Not Break My Strength - Book Talk and Discussion
description:
Sunil Mohan's complex and moving memoir is a deeply felt account of yearning, of knowing with certainty who you wish to be, and of the constant, fundamental questioning of what it means to be born into one body and inhabit an identity defined by a different body and a different set of ascribed and acceptable behaviour.
Asking why he cannot choose to define his gender in his own way, why being 'man' should mean adopting a given, socially acceptable model of masculinity, Sunil writes: "I was always uncomfortable with 'masculinity' even when I deeply felt I was a 'man'….I was hesitant to identify with something I had critiqued so fundamentally."
Honest, open, self-questioning and filled with courage and compassion, Sunil Mohan chooses to move away from the traditional and often linear trajectory of a life narrative. Instead, he turns the lens on the queer, trans, anti-caste, feminist and people's movements of which he has long been a part. In doing so, he resolutely refuses to identify as a victim and thinks through and reflects on the politics of resistance, marking the learning that comes from friendships forged in struggle and commonality of identity, reflecting on the meanings of silence and offering thoughts on strategies for healing and reconciliation.
This book emerged as part of a research study on De-Constructing Masculinities by maraa, a media and arts collective, supported by Edelgive Foundation.
Age: 18+

name: The Art Of Feeling - Art Therapy Workshop
description:
No pressure to talk it out or make sense of it. just sit, pick up a colour, and let things unfold the way they want to.
The Art of Feeling is about giving your mind a break and your emotions a place to land — through colour and conversation.

All materials included.
Age: All

name: Handmade Book Binding - A workshop
description:
In this hands-on workshop, participants will be introduced to the fundamentals of bookbinding. They will learn the basics of the craft, including key terminology, tools, and materials commonly used in traditional bookbinding practices.
The session will focus on creating a handmade book using the Coptic stitch binding technique, a historical and visually striking method known for its exposed spine and ability to lay completely flat when open. Each participant will complete their own book by the end of the session, gaining practical experience in folding, punching, stitching, and assembling the components.
This workshop is ideal for beginners as well as creative enthusiasts interested in crafts, journaling, or book arts.
All materials are included.
 
Age: 16+

name: The Bougainvillea Circle - Connection Circle
description:
"Sawubona" a Zulu Greeting meaning, "We See You". At Bougainvillea Circles you are offered a space to be heard, seen, welcomed as you are. A non-judgmental, safe, trusted space where you are free to be all that you want to be. Nothing More, Nothing less. No Restrictions, no judgements, just an intimate group of people looking for a space to be real. A space where we`re all less alone for a little while. Where we are seen, how we want to be seen. Conversations, Connections, Community of Compassion. All that makes the world go round, after Love of course!
Remember - Sawubona: We See You.

name: Atta Galatta presents 'Alchemy with Art' - A Creative Healing Workshop Guided
description:
Atta Galatta presents 'Alchemy with Art' - a creative healing workshop guided by Harshitha on Easter Sunday 5 April 2026 at 11:30 AM. 
This workshop is in the form of an intimate transformational circle, where you will be guided to use your innate creative superpower to break free from limiting beliefs and unloving self narratives, so that you can rewrite your current story into a beautiful, empowering possibility.
If you've been looking for change, this session is just for you.
Age: 25+

name: Lahe Lahe Caravan presents “Harry Potter Book Discussion” - Meetup
description:
For hard-core Potterheads age 12 and above!
Get into the minds of the characters, ferret out the intricacies of the plot - enter the wizarding world and share your thoughts and insights!
Will help if you have read all the Harry Potter books - this discussion will be based only on the books and not the movies.
The event is curated by Lahe Lahe Caravan and is being hosted at Atta Galatta.
Age: 12+

name: Soul Stories - A Silent Exchange
description:
Sometimes, all we need is for someone to listen. No advice, no judgment—just a space to be heard. 🕊️
Join Sharlin and Sri for "Soul Stories: A Silent Exchange" at Atta Galatta. Bring your story, leave the weight behind, and take a little warmth home with you.
🗓 April 5, Sunday
🕓 4 PM - 7 PM
📍 Atta Galatta, Indiranagar
🎟 Contribution: 250 rs per head (Small support for the community)
Limited spots available. DM to reserve your seat in our sanctuary. ✨
Contact number: 7550126978
Name: sriranjani and sharlin
What to expect: You will have a dedicated space to share your story or thoughts in total silence. We (Sharlin and Sri) are your 'Story Keepers'—there to listen with an open heart. We provide a safe harbor, a warm hug, and a small token of gratitude to take home.
About the Organizers:
Sharlin and Sriranjani are dedicated space-holders and travelers who believe in the healing power of being heard. With a background in community building and creative arts, they created 'Soul Stories' to provide a rare, silent refuge for those looking to share their journey without judgment.
Age: 18+

name: Muqadamma – The Trial - A Poetic Musical courtroom drama of Three Witnesses
description:
Muqadamma unfolds through three witnesses —
one who never spoke,
one who chose silence,
and one who was silenced… until even the ruins began to speak.
Created and performed by Ruha Latif — a practicing civil litigation advocate, a woman’s rights activist and a speaker on gender discrimination and Shariah rights of Women, the author of “Rooh Dekhi Hai Kabhi (Part 1 & 2)”, Ballads from a Mother’s Heart & Conversations, and a performing artist who bridges courtroom and poetry.
Accompanied by live violin and mandolin by Utsav Biswas , he has trained in both Indian and western classical music. He loves to explore how emotions and music intertwine. 
This gathering is more like witnessing a courtroom trial.

Age: all

name: Rainbow Journal - Meet-up and  Workshop
description:
Rainbow Journaling is a creative, reflective workshop that invites you to explore your thoughts, stories, and emotions through colour and words.
Using guided prompts, colours, and simple journaling exercises, this session creates a relaxed space to slow down, reflect, and put your thoughts onto the page. Think of it as a pause from the noise of everyday life — a chance to sit with your ideas, express yourself freely, and see what emerges.
This is not about perfect writing or polished storytelling. It’s about curiosity, creativity, and letting the page hold whatever you feel like expressing.
✨ Guided prompts to spark reflection
✨ Creative journaling using colour and words
✨ A calm, welcoming space to write and think
✨ A chance to explore the many shades of your story
Come as you are... all you need is an open mind and a willingness to write.
Age: 18 and above

name: Indie Authors Social - An Intimate Creator’s Meet Up
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta, right by Indiranagar Metro, for a unique Indie Authors Social! This event is inspired by the real struggles indie authors face. You’ll connect with fellow writers, gain clarity on your 1- to 3-year goals, and share support to collaborate—because no one should have to succeed alone. Building on the success of two Creator Socials, this is our next step in helping authors make their dreams a reality. Let’s rise together!
While there have been author conferences and book fairs, this event stands apart. Unlike large, general gatherings, this Indie Authors Social focuses on intimate, goal-driven collaboration. Drawing on real indie author struggles, it’s not just about networking—it’s about finding partners to help each other succeed. While similar themes exist globally, this rare event offers a local, personal touch—ensuring indie authors don’t walk their journey
Ready to grow your author journey? Click the link in our bio now to secure your spot—spaces are limited, and this year’s event won’t happen again until next year.
 
Detailed flow of the event:
Connection (First 30 mins):
We’ll begin with icebreakers to help authors get to know each other, so they can identify potential collaborators right from the start.
Clarity (Next 30 mins):
Authors will clarify their goals—whether for 1, 2, or 3 years—and reflect on their target audience, their book, and key challenges.
Sharing & Exchange (30 mins):
Attendees will discuss what they need to reach their goals and what they can offer others—building mutual support.
Goal Framework (30 mins):
Authors will outline a simple plan—key steps they need to take and who they might seek help from.
Mastermind Groups (30 mins):
We’ll form groups of four indie authors and one aspiring author, creating supportive circles.
Reflection (Final 30 mins):
We’ll wrap up by reflecting on the event, and each author will commit to what they will achieve in the coming year.
Age: 18+

name: Chocolate & Stories - A Guided Chocolate Tasting and Storytelling Experience
description:
Step into a slow, sensory afternoon of chocolate with AMAY.  
This intimate experience invites you to explore handcrafted chocolates through flavour, texture, and storytelling. As you sample a curated selection of artisanal creations, you’ll discover the nuances of cocoa, the craft behind each piece, and the stories that bring them to life.  
Hosted by Sreya, Founder of AMAY Dessert Studio, this session is designed to be warm, engaging, and experiential — perfect for chocolate lovers and curious minds.  

Age: 16+

name: A Summer of Creativity - 5 day Summer Art Camp
description:
Summer is here… and so is the screen time for kids..
Let’s swap that for creativity 💫
Join Summer Art Camp for a fun-filled 5 days where kids explore different art mediums, express themselves, and take home their own creations 🎨
✨ Canvas painting
✨ Fluid art 
✨ Upcycled coconut shell art
✨ Hand-building with clay
✨Collage making ( Self-portrait )
 
⏰ 11 AM – 1 PM
📍 Atta Galatta
💰 ₹5000 (5 days) | ₹1200 (single day)
🎨 All materials included
Limited spots only!
Register now via the link in AG’s bio 💛
Age: 6-13 yrs

description:
Summer is here… and so is the screen time for kids..

name: The Adventures of Rifa - Book Reading and Signing
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for the book launch of The Adventures of Rifa by Samina Mahmood, a charming children’s book that brings alive the joys, mischief and wonder of growing up in a hill station in the 1950s.
Through the eyes of Rifa—curious, courageous and delightfully mischievous—the book follows a series of small but memorable adventures shared with her brother, cousins and friends. Set against the backdrop of sun-dappled days and rainy afternoons, these stories capture the innocence of childhood while gently reflecting on the lessons, values and friendships that shape us as we grow.
Join us for a warm and engaging session with the author as she introduces young readers to Rifa’s world and shares the memories and inspirations behind the book.

name: Keeping A Sketchbook Diary - an introduction to gouache techniques
description:
Have you ever wanted to capture a moment — not just with a photo, but with your hands? This workshop is an invitation to slow down, look closely, and make something that is entirely yours.
In two hours, you'll move through three acts: first, a set of guided observation exercises designed to train your eye — noticing colour, negative space, and the shapes hiding in plain sight. Then, a reflection practice using poetry, short passages, and journal prompts to find the words for what you've seen. Finally, integration — weaving your sketches and writing into a single page, and learning how to carry this practice home.
No experience needed. Gouache is a forgiving, vibrant medium — bright payoff, slow pace, good company.
All Materials are included.
Age: 16+

name: THE COMMUNITY ART CIRCLE - Wonders Of Team Work In Art And Story Making
description:
A fun take on art with a community team spirit.
Seated in a circle layout, the imagination comes to life with basic art skills, inspiring stories and creative expressions.
Take home a creation inspiring stories, memories and a lot of interpretations!
Art. Story. Repeat!

For Aditi, creativity is a loop of endless possibility: art sparks a character, and story breathes life through imagination.
After beginning her career as a UX/UI Design professional in Bengaluru and pursuing it for more than a decade; she has been pursuing the joy of storytelling through her venture, Band of Beads. Narratives are woven through beading, fabrics, and puppetry - crafting tiny worlds with big hearts.

name: A Summer of Creativity - 5 day Summer Art Camp
description:
Summer is here… and so is the screen time for kids..

Age: 6-13 yrs

name: Mantras of the Moon - Book Launch & Poetry Reading
description:
Atta Galatta invites you to an evening of poetry, conversation, and reflection with poet Srijani Mitra, celebrating the launch of her new poetry collection Mantras of the Moon.
Published by Red Rook Press, Mantras of the Moon is a lyrical exploration of identity, cultural inheritance, memory, and selfhood. Through meditative and evocative poems, the collection navigates questions of belonging, Indianness, spirituality, and the inner landscapes that shape who we become.
In this special launch event, Srijani Mitra will read from the collection, discuss the ideas and inspirations behind the book, and engage in conversation with the audience about poetry, culture, and the creative process.
About the Author:
Srijani Mitra is a poet and writer whose work explores the intersections of culture, selfhood, and language. She has presented her work in academic and literary spaces including a talk organized by Indian Institute of Technology Dharwad. Mantras of the Moon marks an important addition to contemporary poetry engaging with cultural identity.
Join us for an intimate literary evening celebrating poetry and the power of voice.
Age: 16 and above

name: THE ROAR AND THE SQUEAK! - Puppet Show, Art Session And Some Fun-Time
description:
The mighty lion and the tiny mouse return in a charming puppet show filled with sing-along songs and storytelling through handmade puppets. Children will then be guided through an art activity to create an animated lion – the imaginative storytelling version of the character on paper!
A perfect blend of art, imagination, and storytelling: come and create your own jungle tale!

For Aditi, creativity is a loop of endless possibility: art sparks a character, and story breathes life through imagination.
After beginning her career as a UX/UI Design professional in Bengaluru and pursuing it for more than a decade; she has been pursuing the joy of storytelling through her venture, Band of Beads. Narratives are woven through beading, fabrics, and puppetry - crafting tiny worlds with big hearts.
Art. Story. Repeat!

name: Hired. Fired. Tired. - Book Launch
description:
Come join us for an afternoon of fun, banter, empowerment, and honest conversations on womanhood, workplace nightmares, women’s leadership, cognitive biases-and much more.
On May 24, join us at Atta Galatta for the launch of Hired. Fired. Tired. by debut author Tushara Reddy- a candid and thought-provoking book exploring the realities of the modern tech workplace. Framed through the perspective of a first-generation millennial professional, the book unpacks the often-unspoken politics of the industry-where ambition, identity, and survival frequently collide.
Told from a woman’s point of view, Hired. Fired. Tired. speaks to those navigating toxic work cultures, imposter syndrome, layoffs, and the quiet pressures of being a newcomer. At once deeply personal and broadly relatable, the book gives voice to the silent struggles of everyday work life while imagining bold possibilities for a more humane and equitable tech future. Tushara also invites readers to be part of her personal journey of writing toward a more utopian tech world-one where empathy, fairness, and opportunity shape the future of work.
As a bonus, the evening will also feature a panel discussion with global tech leaders, exploring real stories, challenges, and ideas for building healthier workplaces in the tech industry.
Tushara Reddy is a mid-senior product manager with over nine years of experience across India, the UK, and the USA. An alumna of Queen Mary University of London, she currently lives in Bengaluru, where she balances work, workouts, and writing in her spare time.

Age: 18 and above
